title: Call of the Wild
author: Jack London
subline: The classic story of Buck, a dog reborn in the Yukon.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A wolf's howl: a single massive triangular silhouette (the wolf's head) facing up, mouth open. The howl is rendered as concentric arcs from the mouth. A red moon behind. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 950)">
    <!-- Red moon (the only red) -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="-300" r="280" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="-300" r="220" fill="#fc1c46" opacity="0.12"/>
    <!-- Wolf head silhouette — simple polygon profile facing up, mouth open toward the sky -->
    <g fill="#1a1a1a" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1.5">
      <path d="M-120 200 L -100 100 L -50 60 L -30 0 L -10 -100 L 10 -100 L 30 0 L 50 60 L 100 100 L 120 200 L 100 240 L 60 260 L 20 240 L 0 220 L -20 240 L -60 260 L -100 240 Z"/>
      <!-- Open mouth at top -->
      <path d="M-20 0 L -10 -100 L 10 -100 L 20 0 Z" fill="#0a0a0a"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Howl waves from the mouth — concentric arcs -->
    <g fill="none" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1.5">
      <path d="M-100 -160 Q 0 -240 100 -160"/>
      <path d="M-160 -220 Q 0 -320 160 -220"/>
      <path d="M-220 -280 Q 0 -400 220 -280"/>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A wolf silhouette in the upper 2/3 of the canvas, head thrown back, howling at a thin red ring (the moon). The howl is rendered as three concentric arcs. 320px uppercase title sits at the bottom. The single red is the moon — the thing being called. The wild is what's being answered.
